title: Topological lattice gauge theory enriched by non-invertible symmetry
authors: Lea E. Bottini, Clement Delcamp, Edmund Heng, Campbell K. McLauchlan, Dominic J. Williamson
abstract: We use finite group topological lattice gauge theory, also known as the quantum double model, as a lens to explore a notion of topological order enriched by a non-invertible symmetry. For invertible symmetry enriched topological order, there is an established axiomatisation in terms of a G-crossed braided fusion category. We lay the foundations for a generalisation of this notion. By condensing an arbitrary algebra of charges in a quantum double model, we demonstrate that the category of localised excitations in the resulting theory forms a hypergroup-graded extension of the category of deconfined excitations. For every element in the hypergroup, the associated domain wall acts in a typically non-invertible way on these localised excitations. Both this action and the monoidal structure are compatible with the hypergroup grading. The actual categorical action is encoded in a Hopf monad on the category of localised excitations, and gauging the non-invertible symmetry amounts to computing the category of modules over this Hopf monad. Finally, we outline how this framework naturally extends to theories obtained by condensing algebras in a generic string-net model.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous mathematical foundation for topological phases enriched by non-invertible symmetries. It demonstrates how anyon condensation leads to a hypergroup-graded structure and shows that the resulting non-invertible symmetry can be captured using Hopf monads.
main_problem: The paper aims to formalize and generalize the notion of Symmetry Enriched Topological (SET) order to cases where the enriching symmetry is non-invertible, specifically through anyon condensation.
main_result: The authors establish a mathematical framework using hypergroup-graded fusion categories and Hopf monads to describe the localized excitations and the non-invertible symmetry action in condensed topological lattice gauge theories.
method: The study employs the tube algebra approach, the relative Drinfeld center construction, and the theory of Hopf monads to classify excitations and encode symmetry actions.
model_or_system: The primary models are finite group topological lattice gauge theories (quantum double models) and generic string-net models, specifically examining the condensation of an arbitrary algebra of charges.
key_observables: Localized excitations (anyons), domain walls, and the categorical action of the non-invertible symmetry.
important_parameters: The group G (parent theory) and subgroup H (condensed theory), and the hypergroup grading of the resulting theory.
main_assumptions: The construction assumes the condensation of pure charges and relies on the existence of a spherical fusion subcategory within a larger category.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the lattice gauge theory and anyon condensation mechanism, moves to the mathematical classification of excitations using tube algebras, introduces the Hopf monad framework for non-invertible symmetries, provides explicit calculations using the D3 and D6 groups, and concludes with a generalization to generic string-net models.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
